Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User has an Android device with internet access
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), strong password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe)
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to the Sign Up screen.
2. Enter the full name in the "Name" field.
3. Enter a valid email address in the "Email" field.
4. Enter a strong password in the "Password" field.
5. Re-enter the same password in the "Confirm Password" field.
6. Tap the "Sign Up" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message appears. User is redirected to the Login screen.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User has registered and confirmed an account
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com), correct password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to the Login screen.
2. Enter the registered email in the "Email" field.
3. Enter the correct password in the "Password" field.
4. Tap the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully and redirected to the home screen.

Title: Failed Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User has registered and confirmed an account
Test Data: Registered email (e.g., test@example.com), incorrect password (e.g., wrongpass)
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and navigate to the Login screen.
2. Enter the registered email in the "Email" field.
3. Enter an incorrect password in the "Password" field.
4. Tap the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Invalid email or password." User remains on the Login screen.

Title: Add a New Environmental Report with Image and Location
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Report title (e.g., "Plastic Waste"), description (e.g., "Large pile of plastic bottles near the shore"), urgency level (e.g., "High"), image file (e.g., plastic_waste.jpg), geolocation (e.g., 45.4408° N, 12.3152° E)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Add Report" screen.
2. Select the report type (e.g., "Plastic Waste").
3. Enter the report title in the "Title" field.
4. Enter the description in the "Description" field.
5. Select the urgency level from the dropdown menu.
6. Tap the "Add Image" button and select an image from the device gallery.
7. Enable location services and allow the app to access the current location.
8. Tap the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Report is submitted successfully, and a confirmation message appears. The report is visible in the user's report history.

Title: Add a New Environmental Report Without Image
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Report title (e.g., "Illegal Dumping"), description (e.g., "Oil spill near the harbor"), urgency level (e.g., "Critical")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Add Report" screen.
2. Select the report type (e.g., "Illegal Dumping").
3. Enter the report title in the "Title" field.
4. Enter the description in the "Description" field.
5. Select the urgency level from the dropdown menu.
6. Skip adding an image.
7. Enable location services and allow the app to access the current location.
8. Tap the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Report is submitted successfully without an image, and a confirmation message appears. The report is visible in the user's report history.

Title: View Report Details
Preconditions: User has submitted at least one report
Test Data: Existing report (e.g., "Plastic Waste")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Reports" screen.
2. Select the report titled "Plastic Waste."
3. Verify the report details (title, description, urgency level, image, location, and submission date).
Expected Result: All report details are displayed correctly.

Title: Edit an Existing Report
Preconditions: User has submitted at least one report
Test Data: Existing report (e.g., "Plastic Waste"), updated description (e.g., "Large pile of plastic bottles and bags near the shore")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Reports" screen.
2. Select the report titled "Plastic Waste."
3. Tap the "Edit" button.
4. Modify the description to "Large pile of plastic bottles and bags near the shore."
5. Tap the "Save" button.
Expected Result: Report is updated successfully, and the changes are reflected in the report details.

Title: Receive Personalized Notifications for Reported Areas
Preconditions: User has submitted at least one report and enabled notifications
Test Data: User’s reported location (e.g., 45.4408° N, 12.3152° E), new report submitted nearby
Test Steps:
1. Ensure the user has enabled notifications for the reported area.
2. Simulate a new report being submitted near the user’s reported location (e.g., 45.4410° N, 12.3150° E).
Expected Result: User receives a notification about the new report in their area.

Title: Update User Account Settings
Preconditions: User is logged in

Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Settings" screen.
2. Select "Account Settings."
3. Update the email to "newtest@example.com."
4. Update the name to "John Smith."
5. Tap the "Save" button.
Expected Result: Account settings are updated successfully, and a confirmation message appears.

Title: Change Password
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Data: Current password (e.g., P@ssw0rd!), new password (e.g., N3wP@ss!), confirm new password (e.g., N3wP@ss!)
Test Steps:

2. Select "Account Settings."
3. Enter the current password in the "Current Password" field.
4. Enter the new password in the "New Password" field.
5. Re-enter the new password in the "Confirm New Password" field.
6. Tap the "Change Password" button.
Expected Result: Password is changed successfully, and a confirmation message appears. User is logged out automatically.

2. User answers questions (e.g., "How important is water quality data to you?").
3. User submits the questionnaire anonymously.
Expected Result: Responses are collected and stored without user identification. Data is analyzed for trends and integrated into system development.

Title: Verify Offline Report Submission and Sync
Preconditions: App is offline.
Test Data: A new report to be submitted.
Test Steps:
1. User creates and submits a report while offline.
2. User restores internet connection.
3. User checks if the report is synchronized in the system.
Expected Result: Report is saved locally while offline and synced upon reconnection.

Title: Measure Map and Report Loading Time
Preconditions: App is online with stable internet.
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and navigates to the map with reports.
2. Measure the time taken to load the map and reports.
Expected Result: Loading time is ≤3 seconds.

Title: Verify App Compatibility on Android 8.1+
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android devices with versions 8.1 (API 27) and above.
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android device with version 8.1 or higher.
2. Test basic functionalities (e.g., login, report viewing).
Expected Result: App runs without crashes or major UI issues on ≥95% of Android 8.1+ devices.

Title: Verify Maximum Steps for Report Submission
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: A new report to be submitted.
Test Steps:

2. Count the number of steps required to complete the submission.
Expected Result: Report submission is completed in ≤5 steps.

Title: Verify Automated Test Coverage
Preconditions: Access to automated test suite.
Test Data: None
Test Steps:

2. Measure the percentage of code covered by tests.
Expected Result: ≥95% of the codebase is covered by automated tests.

Title: Verify GDPR compliance for user data management
Preconditions: User account exists in the system
Test Data: User personal data (name, email, location history)
Test Steps:
1. User requests deletion of personal data via app settings.
2. System prompts confirmation for data deletion.
3. User confirms deletion.
Expected Result: All user data is permanently deleted from the system, and a confirmation message is displayed. User receives an email confirmation of deletion.

Title: Validate GPS accuracy for user location
Preconditions: GPS permission enabled on the device
Test Data: Known geographic coordinates (e.g., 40.7128° N, 14.2905° E)
Test Steps:
1. User grants GPS permission to the app.
2. App requests and retrieves current GPS coordinates.
3. App displays user location on the map.
Expected Result: Retrieved coordinates match the known location within ±10 meters. Map pin is accurately placed.

Title: Measure response time for map loading
Preconditions: Device connected to stable internet
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and navigates to the map screen.
2. System timer starts upon map screen load initiation.
3. Map fully loads and becomes interactive.
Expected Result: Map loads within 4 seconds. Timer confirms response time ≤4 seconds.

Title: Measure response time for sending a report
Preconditions: User is authenticated and on the report submission screen
Test Data: Valid report details (type, description, coordinates)
Test Steps:
1. User fills out and submits a report.
2. System timer starts upon submission button press.
3. Server acknowledges report submission.
Expected Result: Report is submitted within 4 seconds. Confirmation message appears.

Title: Verify maximum steps for report submission
Preconditions: User is authenticated and on the home screen
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to "Submit Report" option.
2. User fills mandatory fields (type, description, location).
3. User submits the report.
Expected Result: Report submission completes in ≤5 steps. No additional steps required beyond mandatory fields.

Title: Verify system uptime and offline functionality
Preconditions: Device has active internet connection initially
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User submits a report while online.
2. User disconnects from internet (airplane mode).
3. User navigates to the report history.
4. User reconnects to internet.
Expected Result: Report is stored locally and synced upon reconnection. System remains accessible with ≥99.5% uptime.

Title: Verify Android 8.1+ compatibility

Expected Result: App installs and runs without crashes or compatibility errors. All features function as expected.

Title: Verify system saves emergency report in offline mode and sends upon reconnection
Preconditions: Device has no internet connection, user is logged in
Test Data: Emergency report details (e.g., typology, location, description)
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application in an area with no internet connection.
2. User selects the "Crea Segnalazione" (Create Report) option.
3. User fills in emergency report details and submits it.
4. User exits the application and reconnects to the internet.
5. System automatically sends the saved report to the server.
Expected Result: The report is saved locally while offline and successfully transmitted to the server upon reconnection.

2. Launch the application.
3. Navigate to at least one primary function (e.g., Reports, Profile).
Expected Result: The application installs successfully, launches without crashes, and primary functions operate as expected.
